title: --iidfile can write just `sha256:` string, without the actual image id
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When running `podman build` in parallel, it seems that iidfile can end up with
just the string `sha256:` instead of the expected `sha256:IMAGE_ID`. Seems to
require `--squash` and `--timestamp 0` to reproduce, however that might be just
bad luck on my end, since it is some race condition.

I'm pretty sure this is a bug, but any ideas for a workaround? My current
thinking is basically:

	podman build --squash --timestamp 0 . 2>&1 | tail -n1

I wonder if there is a more elegant way or if there are some problems with ^.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

	printf 'from docker.io/library/alpine:3.16\nrun for i in $(seq 0 10000); do touch /$i; done\n' >Containerfile && for i in `seq 1 25`; do podman build --squash --iidfile id.$i --timestamp 0 . & done; wait; ls -al

**Describe the results you received:**

Some id files have 7 bytes (`sha256:`).

**Describe the results you expected:**

All id files should have 71 bytes (`sha256:IMAGE_ID`).

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Every time the number of 7-byte files is different, so seems to be some race
condition. Gets much worse with the size of the image (I cannot reproduce it
without the `touch /$i` in the Containerfile) and with parallelism (happens
very rarely with just 2 or 3 builds in parallel).

**Output of `podman version`:**

	Client:       Podman Engine

	Built:        Thu Jul 21 20:43:18 2022
	OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

comments.body:
Just for the record, my workaround is not working. There is nothing in the
stdout. I assume the underlying cause is the same as for the iidfile being
empty. Snippet from stdout of `podman build`:

	COMMIT
	-->
That smells like the iid file is just a symptom of something else/bigger.

Happens with buildah-commit as well. Current workaround:

	i=0
	while :; do
		: "$((i += 1))"

		img_id=$(buildah commit --identity-label=false --timestamp 0 "$cnt_id")
		[ "$img_id" ] && break

		[ "$i" -lt 30 ] || {
			cat >&2 <<-EOF
	No image ID found after multiple tries. Podman bug strikes again:

	  https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/15162

	Please run once more and hope for better luck.
			EOF
			exit 1
		}
	done
I think so, it seems to work in practice. It is intended to catch the cases when the race condition happens (meaning the img_id is empty) and retry few times.
Ok I thought it was a reproducer.
I was able to reproduce this,  Its a buildah issue and following PR should close this https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4190
